# update-new-model-definitions
Description: Update system configurations across multiple files when new models are introduced, including model lists, context windows, and preferred candidates.
Source: project
1. Identify the names of the new models to be integrated into the system.
2. Locate the primary configuration file responsible for listing available models and add the new model names to it.
3. Identify all secondary configuration files that depend on or reference the model list. These may include files for memory management, heartbeat checks, or other system preferences.
4. For each new model, determine its specific parameters, such as context window sizes, token limits, or preferred usage scenarios.
5. Update each secondary configuration file by adding the new models and their corresponding parameters.
6. Review existing related configurations, such as pricing patterns or compatibility settings, to ensure they align with the new models.
7. Confirm all necessary updates have been applied across all relevant files.
